entries.title: Drone won't arm
symptoms: No response when arm switch toggled
causes: Throttle not at zero / minimum
Accelerometer not calibrated
Angle too steep (drone not level)
RX not bound or no signal
USB cable still connected
CLI 'status' shows arming prevention flags
diagnostics: Check Betaflight OSD or Configurator for arming disabled flags
Run 'status' in CLI — look for 'Arming disabled flags'
Verify throttle channel reads 1000 at idle in Receiver tab
Check accelerometer calibration in Setup tab
fixes: Lower throttle stick fully before arming
Calibrate accelerometer: place quad on flat surface → Setup tab → Calibrate Accelerometer
Bind receiver to transmitter
Disconnect USB before attempting to arm
Set arm switch on a dedicated AUX channel in Modes tab

entries.title: Arming but motors don't spin
causes: Motor protocol mismatch (DSHOT vs PWM)
ESC not receiving signal — bad solder joint on signal wire
Motor output pins damaged on FC
DSHOT setting wrong (e.g. DSHOT600 on slow ESC)
diagnostics: Go to Motors tab → move sliders manually (props off!) — do motors spin?
If sliders work but arming doesn't: check Modes tab arm switch config
If sliders don't work: check ESC protocol in Configuration tab
Measure motor signal pad voltage with multimeter: should toggle between 0.2-0.4V on DSHOT
fixes: Match ESC protocol: Configuration → ESC/Motor Features → Motor Protocol
Try DSHOT300 if DSHOT600 doesn't work
Reflow solder joints on motor signal pads
Check motor resource mapping: CLI 'resource' command

entries.title: Motor stuttering / not spinning smoothly
symptoms: Motor twitches but won't spin up
causes: Bad solder joint on motor wires to ESC
Bridged motor pads on ESC
Motor bell debris / bent shaft
Motor screws too long — touching windings
Damaged MOSFET on ESC
Motor wire insulation rubbing on bell causing short
diagnostics: Swap motor to different ESC pad — if motor works, ESC is bad
Swap known-good motor to suspect ESC pad — if new motor fails, ESC is bad
Spin motor by hand — should spin freely with slight magnetic cogging
Check motor screws are not protruding through motor base
Inspect motor wires for wear where they exit the arm
fixes: Reflow all solder joints on that motor's arm
Check and trim motor screws if too long
Remove motor bell and clean debris
Replace damaged MOSFET (advanced) or replace ESC
Re-route motor wires away from bell contact

entries.title: Motor hot after flight
symptoms: Motor too hot to touch after landing
causes: Props too aggressive for motor KV
PID tune too aggressive (D-term oscillation)
Motor bearings failing
Flying on too many cells for the motor rating
Bent motor shaft causing vibration
diagnostics: Compare motor temps — one hotter than others = individual motor problem
All motors hot equally = tune/prop issue
Check blackbox log for D-term oscillation
Spin motors by hand — grinding = bad bearings
fixes: Use lighter / less aggressive props
Lower D-term gains in PID tune
Replace motor bearings or entire motor
Drop cell count (e.g. 6S to 4S) or lower motor KV
Enable motor temp monitoring if ESC supports telemetry

entries.title: ESC desync / death roll
symptoms: Audible motor stutter during hard maneuvers
100% motor output in blackbox on one motor
causes: ESC timing out of sync with motor
Motor KV too high for ESC timing settings
CPU overload on FC causing DSHOT timing errors
Ramp-up power set too high in BLHeli
Low quality ESC unable to handle current spikes
diagnostics: Enable blackbox logging — look for motor hitting 100% while others normal
Check RPM errors in Motors tab (bidirectional DSHOT required)
Check CPU load in CLI: 'status' — should be under 50%
Reproduce: usually happens on hard maneuvers or punch-outs
fixes: BLHeli: set Ramp-Up Power to 0.125 (BLHeli_S) or 12% (BLHeli_32/AM32)
BLHeli: increase motor timing from Low to Medium or Med-High
Betaflight: lower PID loop frequency (8k/4k → 4k/4k)
Betaflight: switch from DSHOT600 to DSHOT300
Betaflight: set Demag Compensation to High
Add capacitor (25V 1000uF low-ESR) to battery pads
Update ESC firmware to latest BLHeli_32 or AM32

entries.title: ESC magic smoke / burned ESC
causes: Voltage spike from motor braking (no capacitor)
Short circuit from crashed prop hitting wires
Water/moisture ingress
Overcurrent — props too heavy for ESC amp rating
Reversed battery polarity
diagnostics: Visual inspection for scorch marks, popped caps, damaged MOSFETs
Continuity test between VBAT+ and motor pads — continuity = blown MOSFET
Smell test — burned electronics have distinct odor
Check if BLHeli Configurator still detects the ESC
fixes: Replace ESC (4-in-1) or affected arm ESC (individual)
ALWAYS add capacitor to prevent future voltage spikes (25V 1000uF+)
Ensure ESC amp rating exceeds max motor draw by 20%+ margin
Conformal coat ESC for moisture protection
Double-check polarity before connecting battery

entries.title: Motors spin on connect / won't calibrate
symptoms: Motors spin immediately when battery connected
causes: ESC not receiving valid throttle signal
Wrong ESC protocol selected
FC motor output stuck high
ESC firmware corrupted
diagnostics: Listen to startup tones: 3 beeps = power OK. 2 long beeps = signal OK
No second set of beeps = ESC not getting valid signal from FC
Check Configuration tab → ESC protocol matches actual ESC type
fixes: Flash ESC firmware via BLHeli Configurator / Bluejay Configurator
Match protocol in Betaflight to ESC capability
Re-solder signal wire from FC to ESC
If individual ESC: try different motor output on FC

entries.title: Drone flips on takeoff
causes: Wrong motor order in Betaflight
Wrong motor spin direction
Props on wrong motors (CW/CCW swapped)
Gyro orientation incorrect
FC mounted in wrong orientation without board alignment set
diagnostics: Motors tab: verify motor 1-4 match Betaflight diagram
Props off — check each motor direction matches Betaflight diagram
Move quad by hand — verify Betaflight 3D model moves correctly
If model moves wrong: FC orientation or board alignment is off
fixes: Remap motors in Motors tab or CLI 'resource motor'
Reverse motor direction in BLHeli or swap any 2 motor wires
Place CW/CCW props on correct motors per Betaflight diagram
Set Board Alignment in Configuration tab to match FC mounting angle
If FC is rotated 90°/180°/270°: set yaw offset accordingly

entries.title: Betaflight won't connect / DFU mode
causes: Wrong USB driver (need STM32 VCP driver or Zadig for DFU)
Damaged USB connector on FC
FC bricked from bad firmware flash
Wrong COM port selected
USB cable is charge-only (no data lines)
diagnostics: Check Device Manager for unknown USB device
Try different USB cable — many cables are charge-only
Hold boot button while connecting USB to force DFU mode
Check if FC appears in DFU mode in Zadig
fixes: Install STM32 Virtual COM Port driver
Use Zadig to install WinUSB driver for DFU mode
Use known-good data USB cable
Flash firmware via DFU: Betaflight Configurator → Firmware Flasher → DFU
If USB port damaged: solder direct to USB pads or use alternate UART with FTDI adapter

entries.title: Flight controller rebooting mid-flight
causes: Voltage regulator on FC overloaded or failing
Loose battery connector causing power interruption
Solder joint on VBAT pad cracked
Electrical noise from ESC/motors causing brownout
Capacitor missing or failed
diagnostics: Check blackbox for power interruptions (voltage drops to 0)
Wiggle battery connector while connected — does FC reboot?
Inspect all power solder joints under magnification
Measure voltage on 5V and 3.3V regulators during motor spin-up
fixes: Reflow VBAT solder joints
Replace battery lead / XT60 connector
Add low-ESR capacitor (25V 1000uF+) across battery pads
If voltage regulator failed: replace FC or use external 5V BEC
Secure battery with anti-slip pad to prevent connector loosening

entries.title: Gyro drift / toilet bowl effect
symptoms: Drone slowly rotates on yaw without input
Heading slowly changes on its own
causes: GPS rescue or position hold interfering (if GPS present)
Magnetometer (compass) not calibrated or interfered by electronics
I-term windup on yaw axis
Vibrations causing gyro noise
Gyro hardware failing
diagnostics: Fly without GPS features enabled — if fixed, GPS/mag issue
Check gyro traces in blackbox — clean or noisy?
Check if toilet bowl only in GPS modes or all modes
Verify FC is soft-mounted to reduce vibrations
fixes: Disable magnetometer if not needed (CLI: 'set mag_hardware = NONE')
Recalibrate compass away from metal/electronics
Soft-mount FC with rubber grommets
Reduce yaw I-term if excessive
Update Betaflight firmware — newer versions have better gyro filtering

entries.title: No FPV video / black screen in goggles
causes: VTX not powered (check voltage input)
Wrong channel/band on VTX vs goggles
Camera not connected or dead
Antenna not connected to VTX — NEVER power VTX without antenna
Goggles antenna connector loose
diagnostics: Is VTX LED on? If not, power issue
Try channel scan on goggles — find any signal?
Connect camera directly to a monitor via composite if possible
Check VTX wiring: VBAT/5V in, video signal from camera, ground
fixes: Match VTX and goggle to same band/channel (e.g. R1 = Raceband Channel 1 = 5658 MHz)
Verify VTX is getting power — measure voltage at VTX input pad
Re-seat camera connector or re-solder camera signal wire
ALWAYS connect antenna before powering VTX
Try different VTX channel — may be on wrong one after firmware flash

entries.title: Video static / lines / interference
causes: Electrical noise from ESCs/motors coupling into video
Missing or inadequate capacitor on battery input
Ground loop — FPV system not sharing common ground with FC
Camera power not filtered (needs LC filter)
VTX too close to ESC / high-current wires
diagnostics: Does noise change with throttle? If yes, motor/ESC noise
Does noise appear with motors off but battery connected? If yes, switching regulator noise
Try powering camera from separate regulated 5V source
fixes: Add capacitor (25V 1000uF low-ESR) across battery pads
Ensure all grounds connected (FC, ESC, VTX, camera share ground)
Add LC filter to camera power input
Route video signal wire away from motor/ESC wires
Use shielded camera cable if available
Twist camera signal and ground wires together to reduce noise pickup

entries.title: FPV video range poor / breaks up close
symptoms: Video breaks up at short range
causes: Damaged or wrong polarization antenna
VTX power set too low (25mW pit mode still active)
Antenna connector (MMCX/UFL) not fully seated
Multipathing / reflections in environment
Goggles antenna damaged
diagnostics: Check VTX power setting — may be stuck in 25mW pit mode
Wiggle VTX antenna connector — any change?
Try known-good antenna
Test in open field vs urban — significantly better = multipath issue
fixes: Increase VTX power: Betaflight OSD → VTX settings, or SmartAudio/Tramp menu
Use matched circular polarized antennas (RHCP on both VTX and goggles)
Re-seat MMCX/UFL connector — they work loose from crashes
Upgrade to higher-gain goggle antennas (patch + omni combo)
Position VTX antenna away from carbon fiber frame (CF blocks RF)

entries.title: Receiver not binding / no link
symptoms: RX LED solid/off (not blinking bind pattern)
Betaflight receiver tab shows no channel movement
causes: RX and TX on different protocols (ELRS vs CRSF vs SBUS)
RX firmware version doesn't match TX firmware
Binding phrase mismatch (ELRS)
RX wired to wrong UART or wrong pads (TX/RX swapped)
Serial RX not enabled on correct port
diagnostics: RX LED behavior: fast blink = searching. Solid = bound. Off = no power
Betaflight Ports tab: is Serial RX enabled on the UART the RX is connected to?
Betaflight Receiver tab: is protocol set correctly (CRSF for ELRS/TBS, SBUS for FrSky)?
For ELRS: are TX and RX on same firmware version? Check via ExpressLRS Configurator
fixes: ELRS: ensure binding phrase matches on TX and RX (set in ExpressLRS Configurator)
ELRS: update both TX and RX to same firmware version
Check wiring: TX pad on FC goes to RX pin on receiver and vice versa
Enable Serial RX on correct UART in Betaflight Ports tab
Set Receiver tab → Serial Receiver Provider to match your RX protocol

entries.title: Failsafe / signal loss mid-flight
symptoms: Drone drops or RTH activates unexpectedly
causes: RX antenna damaged or positioned inside carbon fiber frame
RX antenna too close to VTX antenna (same polarization interference)
Loose UART connection (intermittent signal)
TX power set too low
Too many obstacles / out of range
diagnostics: Check RSSI/LQ in OSD or blackbox — gradual decline = range. Sudden drop = hardware
Inspect RX antenna for cuts/breaks in active element
Wiggle UART wires while monitoring receiver tab — any dropouts?
Compare signal at same distance with antenna inside vs outside frame
fixes: Mount RX antenna outside/away from carbon fiber frame
Use antenna tube/mount to position antenna optimally
Separate RX and VTX antennas (different polarization or physical distance)
Reflow UART solder joints
Increase TX power on radio module
Configure proper failsafe in Betaflight (drop, land, or GPS rescue)

entries.title: No GPS fix / slow fix
causes: GPS module not wired correctly (TX/RX/5V/GND)
GPS UART not configured in Betaflight Ports tab
GPS protocol mismatch (NMEA vs UBLOX)
GPS antenna facing down / blocked by battery
Flying indoors — GPS requires sky view
diagnostics: Does GPS module LED blink? (usually blinks on fix)
Betaflight GPS tab: does it show any data?
Check Ports tab: is GPS enabled on correct UART?
Configuration tab: GPS protocol set to UBLOX (most common)?
fixes: Wire GPS: TX→RX, RX→TX on a free UART. Power from 5V pad
Enable GPS on correct UART in Ports tab
Set GPS protocol to UBLOX in Configuration tab
Mount GPS on top of quad with antenna facing up (away from electronics)
For first use outdoors: allow 5-10 min cold start for almanac download

entries.title: Battery voltage sag / early low voltage
causes: Battery internal resistance too high (old/damaged cells)
Battery C-rating too low for quad's current draw
Unbalanced cells — one cell sagging faster
Flying too aggressively for battery capacity
diagnostics: Check individual cell voltages after flight (balance port)
Measure internal resistance per cell (good: <15mΩ, bad: >30mΩ)
Compare flight time: new battery vs suspect battery
Check OSD voltage under load vs at rest
fixes: Replace battery if IR is high or cells unbalanced by >0.1V
Use higher C-rating battery
Use higher capacity battery (but heavier)
Storage charge batteries when not in use (3.8V/cell)
Never discharge below 3.5V/cell under load

entries.title: Nothing powers on / dead quad
causes: Blown fuse (if FC has TVS diode / fuse)
Reversed polarity connection (check XT60 orientation)
Broken wire in battery lead
Bad solder joint on main power pads
XT60 connector pins pushed back
diagnostics: Measure voltage at battery pads on FC with multimeter — should read battery voltage
If voltage at pads: FC power regulator is dead
If no voltage at pads: wiring issue between battery and FC
Check battery is actually charged (connect to charger/checker)
fixes: Continuity test from battery connector through to FC pads
Reflow main power solder joints
Check for pushed-back pins in XT60
Replace blown TVS diode or trace the power path
If FC regulator dead: use external 5V BEC or replace FC

entries.title: Oscillation / vibrations in flight
causes: PID gains too high (especially D-term)
Gyro filtering insufficient
Bent prop or motor shaft causing vibrations
FC not soft-mounted
Prop balance off
diagnostics: Blackbox: look for D-term oscillation (high-frequency noise on D trace)
Lower D-term by 20% — does oscillation reduce?
Try new/balanced props — does jello disappear?
Check all motors spin freely without vibration
fixes: Lower D-term gains (start at 80% of default)
Apply RPM filtering if ESC supports bidirectional DSHOT
Use Betaflight's gyro filter sliders (increase filtering)
Soft-mount FC with rubber grommets or O-rings
Balance props or use pre-balanced props (e.g. HQProp)
Use Betaflight 4.4+ preset tune as starting point

entries.title: Arm cracked / frame damage after crash
causes: Impact damage from crash
Carbon fiber fatigued from repeated crashes
Motor bolts too tight causing stress fracture
Design weakness in arm/body junction
diagnostics: Visual inspection — flex each arm looking for cracks
Spin props — wobble visible on one arm?
Check motor mount bolts are tight but not overtightened
Hold arm and wiggle — any play at body junction?
fixes: Replace cracked arm (most frames sell individual arms)
Temporary: super glue (CA) + baking soda for field repair
If body-arm junction: may need full bottom plate replacement
Consider upgrading to thicker arms (5mm+) or deadcat geometry

entries.title: Settings lost after firmware update
symptoms: All settings reset to default after BF update
causes: Normal behavior — major Betaflight versions reset settings
Didn't save backup before flashing
Full chip erase was selected during flash
diagnostics: Check if diff/dump backup exists
Major version jump (e.g. 4.3→4.4) always requires reconfiguration
fixes: ALWAYS backup before updating: CLI → 'diff all' → save to file
After flash: CLI → paste your saved diff (review each line for deprecated commands)
Use Betaflight Presets for quick baseline configuration
For minor updates (4.4.1→4.4.2): settings usually preserved if 'no full chip erase'

entries.title: Barometer / altitude reading erratic
causes: Barometer exposed to prop wash / airflow
Barometer not covered (needs light shield + foam)
Temperature changes affecting pressure reading
diagnostics: Cover baro sensor with dark foam — does reading stabilize?
Compare indoor vs outdoor readings
Check Betaflight sensor tab for baro data
fixes: Cover barometer chip with small piece of open-cell foam
Ensure foam is dark (light can affect some baro chips)
If not using altitude hold: disable baro in Betaflight (saves CPU)
For precision altitude: use GPS altitude instead of baro

entries.title: Post-crash inspection checklist
symptoms: Just crashed — what to check before flying again
causes: Crash impact
diagnostics: 1. Props: check for cracks, chips, bent blades. Replace if any damage
2. Motors: spin each by hand. Gritty/grinding = damaged bearing or bent bell
3. Frame: flex each arm. Check for cracks at motor mount and body junction
4. Camera: check mount and angle. Lens cracked?
5. Antenna: VTX antenna still attached? UFL connectors pop off in crashes
6. Battery: check for puffing/swelling. Smell for chemical odor. If puffy, retire immediately
7. Solder joints: visual check for cracked joints (especially XT60, motor wires)
8. Betaflight: connect and check gyro/accelerometer readings. Calibrate accel if needed
fixes: Replace any damaged props — even small chips affect balance
If motor gritty: replace bearings or motor
Reflow any suspect solder joints
Recalibrate accelerometer after crash
Test arm and briefly hover before full send
